hub_subtitle: Business English para Gestão de Riscos e Finanças

lesson.menu_title: Cause, Effect & Consequences of Risk — Reading an Incident Report
lesson.menu_desc: Reading a risk incident report and explaining the cause-and-effect chain behind a loss: the words of consequence, the connectors that link cause to result, and a full post-mortem to read and talk through -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 10 -- Cause, Effect & Consequences of Risk
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sergio Takashi Tanaka | Lesson 10 -- Cause, Effect & Consequences of Risk

reading.rtitle: Incident Report -- Energy Desk Limit Breach
paras: On Tuesday, the energy desk recorded a loss of 4.2 million reais. The number itself was not the real story; the story was the chain of events behind it. This report explains the root cause and the effects that followed, so the committee can see how a single error grew.

The root cause was simple. A position limit was crossed early in the morning. Because the limit was not checked before the market opened, the exposure kept growing for two hours. As a result, when prices moved against the desk, the loss was already larger than the model allowed.

The first effect was a margin call the same afternoon. But the impact did not stop there. Since two partner funds held almost the same position, they sold at the same time. Consequently, prices fell further, and the desk's remaining collateral lost value. This knock-on effect turned a local problem into a market one -- a small example of contagion.

By Wednesday, the fallout had reached our own liquidity. Owing to the speed of the move, the team had no time to hedge. The lesson is clear: the loss was not caused by one bad trade, but by one missed check that led to a chain of consequences.

gist.prompt: What is the best title for this report?
choices: Tuesday's 4.2 Million Loss: The Final Number
How One Missed Check Led to a Chain of Consequences
A Beginner's Guide to Trading on the Energy Desk

items: The 4.2 million loss was the real story of the incident.
The report says the number was not the real story -- the chain of events behind it was.
The root cause was a position limit that was crossed and not checked.
A limit was crossed early in the morning and was not checked before the market opened.
The two partner funds selling at the same time made prices fall further.
Because they held the same position and sold together, prices fell further -- a knock-on effect.
The team had plenty of time to hedge on Wednesday.
Owing to the speed of the move, the team had no time to hedge.

listenings.text: Hi Sergio, it is Marina. Before the committee meets, here is the short story behind Tuesday's loss. The root cause was a single limit breach on the energy desk. Because that limit was crossed and not checked, our exposure jumped, and as a result the margin call arrived the same afternoon. The loss was not huge on its own, but it amplified fast. Since two partner funds held the same position, the stress spread, and the knock-on effect hit our liquidity by Wednesday. So the impact was much wider than the first number suggested. Please write a clear cause-and-effect summary for the committee, and show how one breach led to the whole chain. Thanks, talk soon.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. A quick note on the fallout from Tuesday. The direct loss was small, but the ripple effect was the real problem. Because the two funds sold at the same time, prices fell further, and consequently our collateral lost value too. That is contagion in one line: one desk's trouble becomes everyone's trouble. Owing to the speed of it, we had no time to hedge. For the report, my advice is simple. Name the root cause first, then follow the effects in order, so the committee sees the whole chain and not just the final number. Copy me when the draft is ready. Thanks.

extra_audio.text: First, name the root cause of the loss. Then show the first effect it produced. Next, link the cause to the result with a connector such as as a result. After that, explain the knock-on effect on the wider desk. Finally, state the full impact and the lesson for next time.
